CENTINEL — OpenTimestamps Merkle Root
Dataset: Honduras 2025 Presidential Election (CNE)
Files anchored: 64 JSON snapshots (2025-12-03 to 2025-12-10)
Merkle root (SHA-256): f17dbfe188d2f1a1b31248a343ae3397984ef2ec6550bd1bcded03d31d2a7780
OTS proof file: MERKLE_ROOT_HN2025.ots
Calendars: bob.btc.calendar.opentimestamps.org, finney.calendar.eternitywall.com, btc.calendar.catallaxy.com
Status: PENDING — Bitcoin confirmation in ~1 block (~10-60 min) after submission (2026-06-12)
Anchoring module: src/centinel/core/anchoring.py

Note (2026-06-12): the .ots committed on 2026-06-07 was malformed (missing the
OpenTimestamps file header — a bug in anchor_snapshot_chain's serialization,
now fixed) and was rejected by `ots verify`/`ots info`. This file is a fresh,
correctly-formatted resubmission of the SAME Merkle root, attested by 3
independent calendars.

Verification:
  pip install opentimestamps-client
  ots verify -d f17dbfe188d2f1a1b31248a343ae3397984ef2ec6550bd1bcded03d31d2a7780 \
      tests/fixtures/hnd_2025/MERKLE_ROOT_HN2025.ots

  (the -d flag verifies the digest directly — no need to have the original
  64 snapshot files on disk to check the Bitcoin anchor status)

Once Bitcoin confirms (check periodically with the command above, or run
`ots upgrade tests/fixtures/hnd_2025/MERKLE_ROOT_HN2025.ots`), the file will
be rewritten in-place with the Bitcoin block proof and `ots verify` will
report "Success! Bitcoin block ... attests existence as of ...".

Reproducibility:
  PYTHONPATH=src python3 -c "
  from centinel.core.anchoring import anchor_snapshot_chain
  r = anchor_snapshot_chain('tests/fixtures/hnd_2025/')
  print(r.merkle_root)  # must equal f17dbfe...
  "
